name: Can I get workers comp for carpal tunnel from my repetitive assembly line job in West Valley City?
description: Yes, if medically proven to arise from repetitive work duties in Utah.[1]

mainEntity.name: Can I get workers comp for carpal tunnel from my repetitive assembly line job in West Valley City?

acceptedAnswer.text: Yes, if medically proven to arise from repetitive work duties in Utah.[1]
acceptedAnswer.description: Yes, Utah workers' comp covers carpal tunnel as an occupational disease from repetitive motion if linked to your job.[1] It must arise out of employment, with medical causation proven.[1] Key factors: Show job risks caused it; on-premises presumption helps; need doctor reports.[1] Report within 180 days, file Form 122, and use approved doctors. Consult an attorney if your claim faces pushback on causation.
